2000-03-01.title: China Dismisses Furor Over Its Taiwan Policy
2000-03-01.abstract: Deputy Prime Min Qian Qichen says new Chinese policy paper on Taiwan, which has prompted furious reaction in US, merely 'reiterates the government's consistent stance' and does not represent change in policy (M)

2000-03-01.title: Afghan Heroin Feeds Addiction In Region, U.N. Report Declares
2000-03-01.abstract: United Nations Drug Control Program report finds that Afghanistan is not only largest producer of opium but is also becoming major manufacturer of heroin, which is contributing to rise in addiction throughout region (M)

2000-03-01.title: EU Takes Steps to Create a Military Force, Without Treading on NATO
2000-03-01.abstract: PARIS—  The European Union plans to unveil an institutional arm to handle defense Wednesday. It will be the first concrete step by Europe's major countries to set up their own military forces as a complement to NATO.

2000-03-01.title: Eichmann Memoirs Released To Assist a Libel Defendant
2000-03-01.abstract: Memoirs of executed Nazi Adolf Eichmann are made public by Israel as evidence for Holocaust denial trial in London; Eichmann, who organized deportation of millions of Jews to death camps during World War II, wrote memoirs in Israeli jail in 1961; historian David Irving is suing Deborah Lipstadt for book she wrote that called him 'dangerous spokesman for Holocaust denial'; photo (M)

2000-03-01.title: Joint Chiefs Chairman Protests Troops' Mission to Kosovo Town
2000-03-01.abstract: Joint Chiefs of Staff Chmn Henry H Shelton, irritated that US troops had to retreat from bottle-throwing mob in Kosovo, tells NATO's supreme commander, Gen Wesley K Clark, not to use US troops outside their designated sector (M)

2000-03-02.title: Money Laundering Prompts U.S. Drive For a Tougher Law
2000-03-02.abstract: Clinton administration will ask Congress for sweeping new powers to combat money laundering in wake of discovery that Bank of New York served as conduit for $7 billion in Russian money, some of which reportedly derived from illegal activitiees; administration will seek authority to ban financial transactions between US banks or brokerage houses and off-shore financial centers; administration also looks for ways to make it harder for drug traffickers to funnel profits through American banks; Treasury Sec Lawrence H Summers will announce proposed legislation on Mar 2; effort to seek new powers is clearly response to situation in Russia, where financial mismanagement has been problem for Clinton administration and International Monetary Fund (M)

2000-03-02.title: Spun Off From Military Laboratories, Fabrics Get Smart
2000-03-02.abstract: LONDON—  Once clothes were simple things, made by dressmakers and tailors from good old-fashioned inanimate textiles, like cotton, silk or polyester. The most that our skirts and shirts ever did was to get themselves creased or be hung up to drip-dry.

2000-03-02.title: Israeli Bill May Hobble Barak Efforts With Syria
2000-03-02.abstract: Israeli government defies Prime Min Ehud Barak's appeals and approves preliminary bill that would make future referendum on peace with Syria extremely difficult to pass; move reveals instability of Barak's broad-based coalition and will certainly complicate his peacemaking efforts at time he is trying to entice Syria back to negotiating table; bill would require absolute majority of all eligible voters, not just those who report to polls, to approve referendum; many doubt it will ever become law, but its initial approval sends signal to Syria, which is already skittish, that Barak may not win public backing for what he promises; Syria is seeking full return of Golan Heights; photo (M)

2000-03-02.title: 11 Big Aid Groups Quit Sudan in Dispute With Rebels on Control
2000-03-02.abstract: Eleven of biggest aid agencies working in continual crisis of war and hunger in southern Sudan have left region following dispute with main rebel group over their operations; dispute centers on document that outlines 'understanding' between agencies and rebel group; rebels had set Mar 1 as deadline for forced removal of any aid group that does not sign paper; agencies say document would have given rebels too much control over their operations; say ordinary Sudanese will suffer if they do not return (M)

2000-03-02.title: In Era of T-shirts, Kurung Survives : Malaysians' Garment of Choice
2000-03-02.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  The advent of modernity in Southeast Asia has done to traditional fashion what fast-food restaurants did to the neighborhood tea stall.

2000-03-03.title: Round, Opulent and Very Young and Hip
2000-03-03.abstract: PARIS—  Ever since queens and courtesans began to emphasize their necks with precious opalescent spheres, pearl necklaces have been every woman's best friend. In the 1930s, Coco Chanel wore her sumptuous rope of natural pearls — the token of a grand duke's affection — mixed with a cluster of costume jewelry, inventing a style that was to become a classic. In the '50s, their image became proper and ladylike. A few seasons ago, John Galliano triggered a new craze for pearls when he dressed up a Dior collection with spectacular pearl chokers.

2000-03-03.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-03-03.abstract: EUROPE    GERMANY: KOHL'S NEW FUND-RAISING -- Former Chancellor Helmut Kohl said he had been collecting cash among business friends to help his conservative Christian Democratic Union pay fines imposed for its illegal campaign financing during his years in power. News reports said he had raised $3 million, enough to cover the financial damage from his admission that he accepted $1 million in undeclared donations. Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's Social Democrats called Mr. Kohl's activity ''incredibly embarrassing.''   Victor Homola    ITALY: SCHOOL COMPROMISE -- After a 50-year political battle, Parliament passed a law granting private schools the same status as public schools and guaranteeing subsidies to poor families who choose private schooling. The Catholic Church, which runs 90 percent of private schools, had lobbied for full state funding. Parliament's compromise failed to satisfy Catholic bishops, who called the new law ''incomplete and ambiguous.''   Alessandra Stanley

2000-03-03.title: Brightening Up By Wrapping Up Shawls Add a Dash of Flair
2000-03-03.abstract: PARIS—  The current craze for pashmina shawls of all styles and prices might in the end prove to be detrimental to the status of cashmere as a luxury product. But its impact on women's wardrobes is likely to be lasting by focusing on an accessory — the shawl — which has become a hot fashion ticket.

2000-03-03.title: Attired in Black Pantsuit, She Takes on Uniform of Power : Hillary Gets Down to Business
2000-03-03.abstract: WASHINGTON—  When Hillary Rodham Clinton made her candidacy for the U.S. Senate official, she did so dressed in a sleek black trouser suit. For the occasion, her blonde hair was swept away from her face in a polished and sophisticated manner. As she stood before a crowd of cheering supporters, she looked very much like the strong, independent career woman that the media have, from the beginning, made her out to be.

2000-03-03.title: Israeli Forces Kill 3 Suspected Terrorists
2000-03-03.abstract: Israeli authorities kill three suspected Palestinian terrorists in a clash in an Arab town in northern Israel; Prime Minister Ehud Barak says raid averts a plan 'to sabotage the peace process' with bomb attacks on Israeli civilians; photos (M)

2000-03-04.title: CLINTON TO SEND CHINA TRADE BILL TO CONGRESS SOON
2000-03-04.abstract: Pres Clinton will send Congress within days bill to permanently normalize trade relations with Beijing, amid signs that support for important trade accord is slipping away; accelerated timetable answers call from pro-trade members of Congress who have urged administration to push for early vote because of mounting evidence that measure will be even tougher sell as election-year politics intensify; opposition of organized labor, which has made defeating bill top priority, noted; even pro-trade Republican leaders report trouble rallying support among members; China's renewed threats to use force against Taiwan and administration's own report on China's deteriorating human rights situation have given fresh impetus to right-wing opponents of measure, as well as to those on left (M)

2000-03-04.title: Visiting Beirut, Arab Leaders Bolster Front Against Israelis
2000-03-04.abstract: Prince Abdullah bin Abdel Aziz of Saudi Arabia ends two-day visit to Beirut aimed at strengthening common Arab front fol.lowing Israel's recent bombing of three Lebanese power plants, breakdown of Israeli-Syrian peace talks and similar impasse in negotiations between Israel and Palestinians; follows flurry of visits by other Arab leaders that have taken place against background of angry polemics between Israel and neighbors, particularly Lebanon; passions have been stirred on both sides by events in recent weeks, which included attacks by Hezbollah guerrillas that killed seven soldiers in southern Lebanon; photo (M)

2000-03-04.title: Dominic Bruce, 84, Briton Who Tried to Escape Nazis 17 Times
2000-03-04.abstract: Dominic Bruce, British bomber pilot who made no fewer than 17 attempts in four years to escape from his German captors at Colditz prison, dies at age 84; photo (M)

2000-03-04.title: World Briefing
2000-03-04.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    WEST BANK: PARLIAMENTARY LEADER MEETS ARAFAT -- Yasir Arafat met the speaker of the Israeli Parliament, Avraham Burg, the highest-level public encounter in a month. Mr. Burg said he presented ''the full commitment of Prime Minister Ehud Barak to accomplish a historic breakthrough and agreement.'' But Palestinians cautioned that the meeting did not represent a resumption of talks, which have been on hold largely because of a dispute over transferring land to the Palestinians.   (Agence France-Presse)

2000-03-04.title: Pinochet Receives Hero's Welcome on Return to Chile
2000-03-04.abstract: Gen Augusto Pinochet receives muted hero's welcome from military command on return to Chile from Britain after 16 months of house arrest; thousands of detractors and supporters stage competing demonstrations on streets of capital of Santiago; he spends first day at military hospital undergoing medical tests; is expected to keep low profile through March 11 inauguration of Ricardo Lagos, first Socialist party president since Pinochet overthrew Salvador Allende Gossens in violent coup in 1973; photo (M)

2000-03-05.title: New Flood Aid to Mozambique, and Some Complaints
2000-03-05.abstract: Many people in Mozambique complain that outside world has taken too long to respond to flooding that has devastated the country and that delay has caused unnecessary deaths; government issued urgent appeal for a low target of $65 million, but has received only about $40 million; American and British military helicopters will arrive in a week to start searching for those still stranded by floods and hauling food and medical supplies to the hundreds of thousands of people left homeless; photo; map (M)

2000-03-05.title: Albanian Village Flees After Serbs and Guerrillas Battle
2000-03-05.abstract: Fire firefight breaks out between Serbian police and Albanian guerrillas fighters in Dobrosin, Albanian village in southern Serbia, causing villagers to flee by car into Kosovo; NATO-led peacekeepers and UN officials are concerned about volatile situation on Kosovo's eastern boundary with Serbia; photos; map (M)

2000-03-05.title: In Mexico, Nature Lovers Merit a Kiss From a Whale
2000-03-05.abstract: Mexico cancels plans with Mitsubishi Corp of Japan to build a gigantic salt plant on shores of Laguna San Ignacio, an inlet in Baja California desert where gray whales give birth; nationalist resistance to environmentalists' protests against the plant have been harder to sustain as Mexico becomes more involved in global economy (M)

2000-03-05.title: Driven by Fear, Colombians Leave in Droves
2000-03-05.abstract: Many people are fleeing Colombia and its political and criminal violence, and many of them are headed for US; exodus is particularly alarming because the people emigrating are highly educated professionals; working-class Colombians are also leavign in growing numbers; photo (M)

2000-03-05.title: Unesco's Fat Gets a Trim And Reform Is in the Air
2000-03-05.abstract: Koichiro Matsuura, new director general of Unesco, dismisses 20 politically appointed advisers and takes other steps to reform organization and perhaps lure United States to it; his predecessors turned organization into a personal patronage machine, neglecting programs and bloating the staffing (M)

2000-03-06.title: China's Fierce War on Smuggling Uproots a Vast Hidden Economy
2000-03-06.abstract: China is engaged in desperate war on smuggling after years of tolerance that has long helped enrich country's prosperous southern provinces at expense of central government coffers, even with cooperation of local officials; campaign, devised to increase revenues and clean up government's tarnished image, resulted in 80 percent increase in tariff income in 1999; has proved to be exceedingly delicate balancing act in part because important regional economies are intertwined with smuggling, and because zealous pursuit of crime often ends up, embarrassingly, at Communist Party's own door; photo (M)

2000-03-06.title: Paris Journal; Those Fickle Aesthetes! It's Time to Erase Graffiti
2000-03-06.abstract: Anti-graffiti teams work streets of Paris, erasing scrawl that in some parts of city covers almost every wall, door and ornate entrance; polls indicate residents no longer harbor fondness for particular art form, ranking it with dog droppings and pollution as their biggest complaints; nation debated beauty of graffiti 10 years ago, with some most prominent politicians defending graffiti writers as artists of pop culture; city, one of world's big spenders in keeping up appearance, is spending additional $12 million annually on eliminating graffiti; photo (M)

2000-03-06.title: Mozambique Floods Recede, but New Dangers Rush In
2000-03-06.abstract: Conditions in refugee camp in Chibuto, Mozambique, for thousands left homeless by calamitous flooding discussed; government's struggle to provide for victims finally begins as flood waters subside; UN reports 250,000 people live in 64 camps nationwide and another 250,000 in devastated villages; relief agencies say shortage of helicopters has made it impossible to deliver enough food and supplies; fear situation in country could worsen if heavy rains return to region, as forecast by South African Weather Bureau; photos; maps (M)

2000-03-06.title: Belgian Stance on Austria Reflects Worry Over 'Haiderization' at Home
2000-03-06.abstract: BRUSSELS—  Under a newly activist foreign ministry, Belgium has taken the lead in the European ostracism of Austria and other "moral" issues, seeking to erase its poor international reputation. But many political analysts here say the rhetoric about the rise of the far right in Austria has much to do with fears that an identical phenomenon may be on the rise here as well.

2000-03-06.title: Colombia And Copters And Clash Over Choice
2000-03-06.abstract: Proposal to sell American-made Blackhawk helicopters to Colombia for use in war against drugs draws attention to lobbying efforts of aircraft's manufacturer United Technologies, Connecticut-based conglomerate that is major military contractor and political contributor, and to political payoff for some of state's Democratic legislators; illuminates political dynamics of what would be Washington's first large-scale involvement in civil conflict in Latin America since cold war, initiative that has brought unlikely alliances on every side; photo (M)

2000-03-07.title: Pollution and Water Diversion Blot the Murray : In Downstream Australia, This River Is Running Dry
2000-03-07.abstract: TOM GROGGIN, Australia—  Bouncing over rocks, the water in the Murray River near its source high in the forested mountains of southeast Australia has the same kind of crystal clarity as the call of the magpies and currawongs that echo through the air.

2000-03-07.title: World Briefing
2000-03-07.abstract: THE AMERICAS    CHILE: PINOCHET IMMUNITY CHALLENGED  Judge Juan Guzman Tapia requested that the Appeals Court revoke Gen. Augusto Pinochet's immunity so that he can proceed with indictments against the former dictator involving charges of torture, kidnappings and executions. Should the Appeals Court agree, General Pinochet's lawyers can be expected to appeal the decision to the Supreme Court. General Pinochet has been a Senator for Life, a position that grants him broad immunity from prosecution, since 1998.    Clifford Krauss (NYT)    MEXICO: TAKEOVER AT UNIVERSITY  About 60 hooded protesters carrying clubs and rocks took over the building that houses the offices of the president of the National Autonomous University of Mexico in Mexico City. The demonstrators, who broke windows and cut through steel bars to enter the building, demanded the release of dozens of students jailed after the police ended a 10-month strike at the university on Feb. 6.    Julia Preston (NYT)    EUROPE    RUSSIA: COURT RULES ON ZHIRINOVSKY  The Supreme Court ruled that the ultranationalist leader Vladimir Zhirinovsky must be allowed to run in presidential elections on March 26 despite alleged financial irregularities. Mr. Zhirinovsky had been barred from the race by the Central Election Committee for allegedly misreporting his assets. A court spokeswoman said the judge had not disclosed the reason for the reversal.   (AP)    RUSSIA: BLAIR TO VISIT  Prime Minister Tony Blair of Britain will on Friday become the first Western leader to visit Russia since Acting President Vladimir Putin took office. A spokesman for 10 Downing Street said the agenda includes Russia's military campaign in Chechnya. ''Nobody is pretending we like what is going on there,'' he said, adding that Mr. Blair will argue that ''these problems cannot be solved by force without a political process.''    Warren Hoge (NYT)    BRITAIN: 'RED KEN' BREAKS PLEDGE  Ken Livingstone, a Labor left winger known as ''Red Ken,'' defied Prime Minister Tony Blair by declaring himself an independent candidate for mayor of London. He acknowledged that he was breaking a pledge not to break ranks with his party but said that he was justified because the choice of the official Labor candidate in the May 4 election, former Health Secretary Frank Dobson, was ''rigged.'' Mr. Blair said Mr. Livingstone as mayor would be a ''a disaster in terms of crime and police and business.''    Warren Hoge (NYT)    SWITZERLAND: VOTE ON U.N.  Advocates of joining the United Nations have collected enough signatures -- more than 125,000 -- to force a popular vote on membership. The last effort, in 1986, was resoundingly defeated even though Geneva is the United Nation's European headquarters. A nationwide vote will be held within three years.    Elizabeth Olson (NYT)    SPAIN: REBELS SUSPECTED IN BOMBING  A car bomb exploded in the northern Basque city of San Sebastian, injuring two Civil Guard officers and three other people, Spanish news media reported. The blast, six days before the general elections on Sunday, was blamed on the separatist group E.T.A., which is also blamed for two car bombings since January that have killed three people.    Al Goodman (NYT)    GREECE: PROSTITUTES PROTEST  Some 200 prostitutes demonstrated in Athens, demanding that the Socialist government repeal a law barring renewal of their licenses that was intended to clear the city of brothels ahead of the 2004 Olympics. The prostitutes vowed to march to the Prime Minister's office on Tuesday to press their demands.    Anthee Carassava (NYT)    ASIA    VIETNAM: CELEBRATIONS BEGIN  Vietnam began two months of celebrations commemorating the 25th anniversary of the end of the Vietnam War, which came with the fall of Saigon on April 30, 1975. Washington and Hanoi normalized relations in June 1995 and William Cohen will become the first Defense Secretary to visit since the end of the war when he arrives on March 13 for talks on greater cooperation.   (AP)    INDIA: CLINTON'S ITINERARY  Ambassador Richard Celeste said President Clinton would visit New Delhi, Agra -- site of the Taj Mahal -- Jaipur, Hyderabad and Bombay on his visit to India from March 21 to 25. He will land in New Delhi on the evening of March 19, then fly to Dhaka, Bangladesh, the next day. The envoy said it was not clear yet whether the president would visit Pakistan.   Celia W. Dugger (NYT)    INDIA: COPING WITH TB  In India, where two million people develop tuberculosis each year and nearly 500,000 die from it, an intensive treatment program has saved more than 40,000 lives and achieved cure rates of more than 80 percent, according to a review by a team of 25 national and international experts.   Hari Kumar (NYT)    INDIA: DEATH ON THE ROADS  About 70,000 Indians die and more than one million are seriously hurt each year in road accidents caused by faulty vehicles, bad roads, improper traffic controls and other problems, according to Dinesh Mohan, secretary general of an Asian conference on injury prevention in New Delhi sponsored by the World Health Organization.P. J. Anthony (NYT)    INDONESIA: VIOLENCE IN ACEH  Soldiers burned down 15 shops, two houses and a village council office after searching unsuccessfully for insurgents as 27 families fled their homes in the strife-torn province of Aceh, authorities said. It was the latest in a string of violent outbursts in the oil-rich region on the northern tip of Sumatra, about 1,100 miles northwest of Jakarta. (AP)    MIDDLE EAST    YEMEN: POLE TELLS OF KIDNAPPING  Ambassador Krzysztof Suprowicz of Poland, who was kidnapped in Yemen on Wednesday and released unharmed on Sunday after being held for four days by armed tribesmen, told Polish public radio yesterday that he had gone on a hunger strike after he was abducted. ''The aim was to show to these proud and dignified people that they had no power over me and my body, that in fact it was me who would decide the end of the operation,'' he said. The ambassador was freed after the government deployed troops and issued an ultimatum to the hostage-takers, who had wanted to trade him for one of their leaders held by the government.   (Agence France-Presse)    IRAN: PRIVATIZATION BLOCKED  Parliament has endorsed a move by a clergy-based council to block President Mohammad Khatami's plan to revive the economy by ending a state monopoly on banks and major industries. The shift came after the Guardian Council, which reviews parliamentary legislation, rejected most privatization proposals under Mr. Khatami's five-year development plan as unconstitutional. (Reuters)    AFRICA    ZIMBABWE: MORE FARMS OCCUPIED  Squatters occupied at least 214 white-owned farms across Zimbabwe yesterday, defying orders by the police to return home. The squatters are angry about the rejection in February of a new Constitution that would have permitted seizure of white-owned farms without compensation. President Robert Mugabe plans to reintroduce the land provision in 30 days as an amendment to the current Constitution.   (AP)    RWANDA: AIDE AMBUSHED  An adviser to President Pasteur Bizimungu was killed in his car outside his home in the capital, Kigali. The adviser, Assiel Kabera, had been a prominent figure among survivors of the 1994 genocide -- in which at least 500,000 ethnic Tutsi and moderate Hutu were killed by Hutu extremists -- in western Rwanda.    Ian Fisher (NYT)    SUDAN: U.S. ENVOY VISITS  It was a ''mistake'' that Sudanese warplanes bombed a school last month, killing 14 children and a teacher in the rebel-controlled Nuba mountains, the nation's justice minister, Mustafa Osman Ismail, told the United States envoy to Sudan, Harry Johnston. Sudan is under criticism for the bombing of a hospital on March 1 that killed at least two people. Government planes yesterday dropped two bombs on the compound of the Irish aid group, Concern Worldwide, in southern Sudan.    Ian Fisher (NYT)

2000-03-07.title: U.N. Council Urged to Debate Political Future of Kosovo
2000-03-07.abstract: Bernard Kouchner, director of UN administration in Kosovo, urges Security Council to debate political future of Serbian province, where local elections are scheduled for before end of 2000; invites 15-member council to travel to area to see situation on ground (M)

2000-03-07.title: 2 Kohl Party Contenders Have Strong Ties to Ex-Communist Region : German Politics Opens Up to the East
2000-03-07.abstract: FRANKFURT—  In what could herald a growing role for Eastern Germany, both front-runners for the chairmanship of the tainted Christian Democratic Union are from the former Communist region.

2000-03-07.title: Israel Paroles Settler Who Killed an Arab
2000-03-07.abstract: Yoram Skolnik, Jewish settler sentenced to life imprisonment for execution-style killing of Palestinian Musa Abu Sabha in West Bank in 1993 is paroled after seven years in jail; decision continues pattern fo leniency for Jews convicted of killing Arabs (S)

2000-03-08.title: Schroeder's Previous Nominee Withdraws : New German Candidate Proposed to Lead IMF
2000-03-08.abstract: FRANKFURT—  Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder nominated Horst Koehler, the president of the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, to head the International Monetary Fund after the withdrawal Tuesday of his initial candidate, Caio Koch-Weser.

2000-03-08.title: Westerners Safe, Say Pakistan Fighters
2000-03-08.abstract: Harakat ul-Mujahedeen, Islamic fundamentalist group in Peshawar, Pakistan, boasts that its volunteers have taken part in raids on Indian outposts in Kashmir; State Dept has classified group as terrorist outfit; map; photo (M)

2000-03-08.title: Ljubljana Journal; Like Switzerland, but Without the Cuckoo Clocks
2000-03-08.abstract: Tiny Slovenia is ranked behind Hungary on list of Eastern European countries that are ready to join European Union; since it broke away from Yugoslavia in 1991, its gross domestic product per person has doubled to $10,000, outstripping that of Hungary and rivaling those of poorest European Union members, Greece and Portugal; map; photo (M)

2000-03-08.title: Woman Accuses Israeli Transport Minister in Sex-Assault Case
2000-03-08.abstract: Israeli Transportation Min Yitzhak Mordechai takes leave of absence while police investigate complaint that he sexually assaulted woman who works in his ministry (M)

2000-03-08.title: Dow Drops 3.7% on 'Weird' Day for Markets
2000-03-08.abstract: NEW YORK—  The stark contrast between the "new economy" and the "old economy" was evident on Tuesday as technology stocks rose to touch an important milestone and blue-chip stocks plunged 3.7 percent.

2000-03-09.title: Port of Spain Journal; Taking Care of Those Who Take Carnival Too Far
2000-03-09.abstract: Carnival season in Port of Spain, Trinidad, is busy time for local drug rehab program and for hospital emergency room; most people celebrate without overdoing it, but others lose themselves in sensual smorgasbord and get carried away; photo; map (M)

2000-03-09.title: Biden Joins G.O.P. in Call for Delay in Missile-Defense Plan
2000-03-09.abstract: Sen Joseph R Biden Jr warns Pres Clinton that he should leave decision on deploying national missile-defense system to next administration; call from Biden, senior Democratic senator, adds weight to growing bipartisan movement; Biden disagrees with White House advisers who say Vice Pes Al Gore and Democrats need to proceed with system to inoculate themselves against Republican criticism of being weak on national defense (M)

2000-03-09.title: Clinton Sends to a Wary Congress A Long-Delayed China Trade Bill
2000-03-09.abstract: Pres Clinton sends Congress bill to usher China into World Trade Organization; claims opening China's markets will undermine Communist Party control; long-delayed transmission of bill to Congress sets off battle within Democratic Party that intertwines politics of trade, presidential election and Clinton's final effort to recast relations with Beijing; Clinton is dependent on support from House and Senate Republicans to pass measure; Vice Pres Gore, seeking to appease labor unions that vociferously oppose deal, has said he would negotiate something better if elected to office; Gov George W Bush supports China's entry into WTO, but has not given his position on tying passage of bill to providing new arms for Taiwan, issue several Republicans are pressing; Clinton, in speech at Johns Hopkins University, says little about Taiwan, whose tense relations with China have been worsening; photo (M)

2000-03-09.title: Afghanistan's Girls Fight to Read and Write
2000-03-09.abstract: Afghanistan's Taliban rulers are relenting somewhat on their ban of schooling for girls; schools of varying sizes are opening in homes, some secretly, others discreetly; United Nations estimates that 10,000 young girls are going to classes in Kabul, and more all the time; women operating schools secretly rely on word-of-mouth to attract students; most girls are eager; one teacher notes country's young girls have lost four years of education, and that children with illiterate parents have suffered most; photo of one girl who dressed herself as boy to attend school (M)

2000-03-09.title: India Isn't Happy at Clinton's Changed Itinerary, but Pakistan Is
2000-03-09.abstract: Pres Clinton's decision to call briefly on Pakistan after his five-day tour of India dismays Indian officials but delights Pakistanis; White House officials say purpose of stop in Pakistan is not to sanction coup, but to deliver message to military rulers that they should restore democracy, ratchet down tensions with India and ban Islamic fundamentalist group that United States has accused of terrorism (M)

2000-03-10.title: Kohl Says He'll Pay Fines in Party Fund-Raising Scandal
2000-03-10.abstract: Helmut Kohl, former German chancellor, emerges from weeks of seclusion to announce that generous friends and a mortgage on his house had enabled him to raise enough money to cover financial damage suffered by Christian Democratic party as result of his 'mistakes'; breaks his silence at news conf where he brandishes list of 30 friends and admirers--including actresses, film producers and businessmen--who he says have contributed to raising of $2.95 million; Kohl's actions have exposed party to fines of $3.15 million and set off crisis of dominant political force in postwar Germany; photo (M)

2000-03-10.title: Boat People Losing (Uneasy) Haven
2000-03-10.abstract: Hong Kong announces that it will close its last remaining camp for Vietnamese refugees, ending drama that began in May 1975, month after fall of Saigon, when first boat laden with refugees came ashore in Hong Kong; about 224,000 Vietnamese refugees came to Hong Kong; most of these went on to settle in United States, Canada and other countries; at its peak in 1979, there were 68,695 refugees crowded into camps throughout colony; photo (M)

2000-03-10.title: Bradley Out; McCain Yields : Challengers Quit Presidential Race
2000-03-10.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Former Senator Bill Bradley of New Jersey and Senator John McCain of Arizona, two insurgents whose candidacies had made the 2000 primary contests among the most passionately fought in years, bowed out Thursday, but neither offered a full endorsement to his victorious rival.

2000-03-10.title: C.I.A. Gives Fired Official a Career Award
2000-03-10.abstract: CIA awards career achievement medal to Terry Ward, former senior official who was fired in 1995 for failing to notify Congress about human rights abuses in Guatemala (S)

2000-03-10.title: Indonesian Describes Sectarian Massacre at His Factory : The Man Who Outran Death
2000-03-10.abstract: AMBON, Indonesia—  Agus Lekatompessy survived the massacre at the Wainibe plywood factory because he ran faster than the mob with machetes.

2000-03-11.title: China Asks to Halt Arms Sales to Taiwan
2000-03-11.abstract: China Foreign Min Tang Jiaxuan blames United States for creating tensions between Taiwan and China and demands that it halt all weapons sales to Taiwan, news conf, Beijing (S)

2000-03-11.title: Lipizzaner Stallions Dance Into Trade Brawl
2000-03-11.abstract: If one-trick ponies dream, they dream of being Lipizzaners.     But in the age of globalization, even dreams of white horses need their fine print scrutinized by lawyers. Both the European Union and the World Trade Organization have been drawn into a dispute over what exactly makes a horse an official descendant of those first bred in this mountain hamlet in 1580.

2000-03-11.title: NATO Chief in Kosovo Fears a Cross-Border Insurgency
2000-03-11.abstract: Gen Klaus Reinhardt, commander of NATO peacekeeping troops in Kosovo, with former political leader of disbanded Kosovo Liberation Army beside him, vows to stop any insurgency mounted from Kosovo into Serbia; warning comes at joint news conference, with Reinhardt joined by Hashim Thaci, Albanian leader; Albanian leaders warn that tension in Presevo Valley between ethnic Albanian population and Serbian police forces is only going to worsen; photo; map (L)

2000-03-11.title: Former Turkish Leader Gets 1-Year Sentence for 1994 Speech
2000-03-11.abstract: Turkish court sentences former Prime Min Necmettin Erbakan to one year in prison for speech he gave in 1994, even as European officials renew their calls on Turkey to become more democratic; several European leaders have come to Turkey in recent weeks to warn that country has no hope of entering European Union unless it repeals laws like the one under which Erbakan was convicted (M)

2000-03-11.title: Putin Tells Why He Became a Spy
2000-03-11.abstract: Acting Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia says he was smitten with romance of working for secret service when he saw The Sword and the Shield, 1968 movie depicting the heroic deeds of a Soviet double agent in Nazi Germany (M)

2000-03-12.title: Spain Turns Cool to the Chief Who Engineered Its Boom
2000-03-12.abstract: Prime Min Jose Maria Aznar's re-election campaign has failed to stir many Spaniards despite country's booming economy; some voters think he is too far to right in country that, after decades under Franco dictatorship, tends to lean left; polls show that Aznar leads Socialist candidate, Joaquin Almunia, by just four percentage points; photos (M)

2000-03-12.title: Opposition, and International Monitors, Crying Foul in Peru's Presidential Campaign
2000-03-12.abstract: Eight opposition candidates who are running against Pres Alberto K Fujimori in April 9 election are complaining about openness and fairness of electoral process; map; photo (M)

2000-03-12.title: U.S. Begins Lonely Fight To Cut Back Its U.N. Costs
2000-03-12.abstract: US begins long, hard battle to lower its payments to United Nations, but diplomats say there is no guarantee that issue will be resolved; outcome will be crucial to future relations between UN and Washington; what hangs in balance is $926 million in US arrears that has been held up by Congress (M)

2000-03-12.title: Strained Ties: I.M.F. Issue Divides U.S. and Germany
2000-03-12.abstract: US rejection of German Deputy Finance Min Caio Koch-Weser as new managing director of International Monetary Fund has caused serious damage to critical German-US relationship and revealed deep personal rifts within governing coalition of Chancellor Gerhard Schroder (M)a

2000-03-12.title: Taiwan's Open Wallets Bring Fear of a Bought Presidency
2000-03-12.abstract: Taiwan's Open Wallets Bring Fear of a BJ

2000-03-13.title: Gunman in Iran Seriously Wounds a Top Reform Figure
2000-03-13.abstract: Saeed Hajjarian, close associate of Iran's reformist president Mohammad Khatami, who was one of main organizers of the reformers' landslide victory in recent parliamentary elections, is shot in face and critically wounded by unidentified gunman riding on back of motorcycle in Tehran; is reportedly fighting for life with bullet lodged in neck; government says it knows identities of assailants, who escaped; attack raises doubts about ability of reformers to carry out program of broader democracy and respect for human rights in face of bitter opposition from stalwarts of system of Islamic rule; photo (M)

2000-03-13.title: Fiat and GM Are Poised to Form Alliance
2000-03-13.abstract: ROME—  General Motors Corp. and Fiat SpA are expected to announce a strategic alliance and stock swap in the auto sector, which would end the Italian company's long isolation as a lone carmaker lacking the critical mass to prosper on global markets.

2000-03-13.title: American Topics : Short Takes
2000-03-13.abstract: Grandmother is sometimes the necessity of invention. Just ask the young inventors whose works are currently on display at the National Museum of American History.

2000-03-13.title: Aide to Albright Warns the Kosovo Albanians
2000-03-13.abstract: State Dept spokesman James P Rubin arrives in Kosovo, Serbia, for three-day visit; warns Albanian leaders they are losing respect of backers in Washington and Europe and risk forfeiting Western support if they do not do more to stem ethnic violence (S)

2000-03-13.title: Pope Meets Jewish Link to His Boyhood
2000-03-13.abstract: Pope John Paul II holds private audience with Ron Balamuth, New York psychiatrist and grandson of Yechiel Balamuth, who was landlord of Pope and father when both lived in Wadowice, Poland, before World War II; nostalgically pointed out boyhood home during 1999 visit, explaining that it belonged to Jewish landlord who may have died in Holocaust; Yechiel Balamuth died in concentration camp (M)

2000-03-14.title: Albright Aide Cautions Both Serbs and Albanians
2000-03-14.abstract: James P Rubin, State Department spokesman, delivers message from US Sec Madeleine K Albright to Albanian and Serbian leaders during three-day visit to Kosovo; warns against playing into hands of Pres Slobodan Milosevic and urges rejection of extremists who are behind recent violence (S)

2000-03-14.title: Trial Opens on Serb General Accused in Srebrenica Massacre
2000-03-14.abstract: Trial of Gen Radislav Krstic, Serb general accused of overseeing largest single massacre of Bosnian war begins in The Hague; prosecutor says massacre in Srebrenica may have been prevented if there had been determined resistance from UN peacekeepers (S)

2000-03-14.title: Protests on New Genes and Seeds Grow More Passionate in Europe
2000-03-14.abstract: Article in series, Redesigning Nature, examines ongoing debate over genetically modified foods that has grown more passionate and more intense in Europe compared with US, where nearly two-thirds of products on supermarket shelves contain genetically altered ingredients, and few Americans seem to notice; debate may have progressed beyond level of rational discussion in Europe, where increasing numbers of retailers have banished products from shelves; American farmers remain undeterred as big food processors have agred to buy modified grains and keep them separate from traditional ones; focus of European fury is technology that borrows genetic code from plants and animals and transfers it to plant to make it resistant to pests and increase crop yields; while opponents concede no one has been harmed by genetically modified foods, they argue that environmental threats are real and that more research is needed on long-term safety; European opposition attributed to myriad factors, including jingoism about food, scare campaigns by environmentalists, arrogant American responses and anti-Americanism; photo (M)

2000-03-14.title: Mortars Fired in Iran Near a Military Base
2000-03-14.abstract: A day after a democratic reformer was wounded in a shooting in Tehran, the Iranian capital was the scene of another attack when mortar shells were fired into a residential complex near a military base.

2000-03-14.title: Taiwan Stock Market Plummets Over Election Jitters
2000-03-14.abstract: Fears that Taiwan's governing Nationalist Party may lose closely fought presidential election on March 17 causes largest single-point loss ever and largest percentage decline in decade on Taipei stock exchange; main stock index plummeted 617 points, or 6.6 percent, to 8811.95; three main candidates are in dead heat in island's most competitive race; photo (M)

2000-03-15.title: U.S. to Drop Longtime Ban On Luxuries From Iran
2000-03-15.abstract: Clinton administration is about to ease import bans on some Iranian consumer goods to encourage reformers in Iran who recently won victory in elections for Parliament (M)

2000-03-15.title: Israeli Jets Pound Targets in Lebanon for Second Day
2000-03-15.abstract: Israeli jets attack targets in Lebanon for second day in row following Arab League meeting in Beirut and its harsh denunciation of Israel; photo (M)

2000-03-15.title: Montreal Journal; Pokemon Wins a Battle but Not the Language War
2000-03-15.abstract: Battle to get French-language Pokemon cards for children in Montreal and legal battles brewing over language laws reflect fact that Quebec's French-speaking majority continues to view itself as oppressed minority; photos (M)

2000-03-15.title: From Minks to Mules, U.S. Issues China Trade Details
2000-03-15.abstract: Clinton administration, responding to Congressional pressure, declassifies and releases in full trade agreement it reached with China last year; 250-page document enshrines China's commitment to allow capitalism to spread; photo (M)

2000-03-15.title: U.N. Chief Assesses Benefits to Iraq of Oil-for-Food Program
2000-03-15.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan report to Security Council finds that Iraq has raised over $13 billion for food, medicines and other vital supplies under United Nations plan that allows it to sell some oil under international sanctions (M)

2000-03-16.title: Ukraine Leader Cancels U.S. Visit Over I.M.F. Complaint
2000-03-16.abstract: Ukraine Prime Min Viktor Yushchenko cancels visit to US after Clinton administration tells him that he should come only after 'cleaning up' his government, which was discovered to have abused major international loan program; International Monetary Fund says Ukraine supplied false information about foreign reserves from 1996 and 1998 (M)

2000-03-16.title: Paris Journal; A Mayor Fights for City Hall, and Its Foie Gras
2000-03-16.abstract: Paris Mayor Jean Tiberi wants to run for re-election in 2001 despite polls that show only 5 percent of Parisians believe he is honest; photo (M)

2000-03-16.title: Diamonds-for-Arms Report Is Challenged in Security Council
2000-03-16.abstract: Dozen African and European nations challenge Security Council report on how sanctions against rebel army in Angola are being broken; report describes methods that rebels have used to smuggle Angolan diamonds out for sale, enabling them to buy weapons to sustain decades of civil war (M)

2000-03-16.title: Study Shows 45% of Swiss Believe No Apologies to Jews Are Necessary
2000-03-16.abstract: GENEVA—  In the wake of two reports reproaching Switzerland for its treatment of Nazi victims' assets and of Jews fleeing Germany, a study made public on Wednesday said that 45 percent of Swiss citizens believe their country had "nothing to apologize about for its behavior to Jews" during World War II.

2000-03-16.title: U.S. Troops Seize Weapons From Albanians in Kosovo
2000-03-16.abstract: US troops in Kosovo carry out raids on strongholds of ethnic Albanian guerrillas, capturing bombs and other weapons in largest effort to date by US forces to end guerrillas' threat to NATO-led peacekeeping mission; French peacekeepers also clash with Serbian protesters in Mitrovica; NATO annouces addition of 1,100 troops in Kosovo; new troops, 700 French and 400 Italian soldiers, will beef up security forces in Mitrovica; map; photo (M)

2000-03-17.title: Pentagon Balks at Adding Kosovo Battalion
2000-03-17.abstract: Gen Wesley K Clark, top NATO commander, wants United States to commit additional battalian of 700 to 800 soldiers for Kosovo to prevent Albanian raids across border into Serbia, but Pentagon balks at idea (M)

2000-03-17.title: Major Overture Toward Iran Expected in Speech by Albright
2000-03-17.abstract: United States Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright will make a major overture to Iran on March 17 by announcing that Clinton administration will lift several sanctions on Tehran; administration officials say she will also acknowledge that United States was shortsighted in its support for Iraq during Iran-Iraq war and its involvement in 1953 coup in Iran (M)

2000-03-17.title: Suddenly, Reclusive North Korea Reaches Out to the World
2000-03-17.abstract: North Korea, in space of a few short weeks, has gone from one of world's most politically reclusive states to most diplomatically ambitious; with little fanfare, but with speed that has surprised many people, Communist government in Pyongyang has established diplomatic relations with Italy and has resumed diplomatic discussions with Japan and Australia for first time in years (M)

2000-03-17.title: Fate of 5 Terrorists Hangs Between Japan and Lebanon
2000-03-17.abstract: Japan seeks extradition from Lebanon five Japanese Red Army terrorists who were involved in massacre at Israel's international airport near Lod on May 30, 1972; terrorists were sentenced to life in prison by Israel, but they were released by Israel in 1985 in prisoner exchange with Palestinians in 1985; they have been in prison in Lebanon for three years for living on forged passports; Japan wants to extradite Mariko Yamamoto, one of terrorists, to face trial for terrorism and acts against the state at home, and Lebanon, bowing to popular feelings, rejects Japanese request; photo of Red Army terrorists (L)

2000-03-17.title: A Russian Press Beholden to Many
2000-03-17.abstract: Russia's regional newspapers are increasingly under thumb of local authorities; this has not escaped attention of Acting Pres Vladimir V Putin, passionate advocate of building a strong federal state in a Russia whose vast and distant lands have slipped from the authority that Moscow enjoyed in Soviet times; one of Putin's first decrees as acting pres authorizes federal subsidies to 2,500 local newspapers, including 19 provincial newspapers; photo (M)

2000-03-18.title: A Calling of Faith and Art in a Russian Church
2000-03-18.abstract: Rev Andrei Davydov is using his art to finance restoration of 12th century church in Pskov, one of oldest cities in all Russia; Cathedral of the Nativity of St John the Baptist, which dates to 1124, was in shambles when Russian Orthodox patriarchate regained it in 1991; Rev Davydov, who has worldwide reputation for his painted icons, now accepts commissions from other churches to pay for his church's repairs and rebuilding; photos; map (M)

2000-03-18.title: World Briefing
2000-03-18.abstract: Judge in Zimbabwe rules in favor of white farmers, saying black squatters on their farms are breaking law; squatters, veterans of Zimbabwe's war of independence, had invaded hundreds of farms to speed pace of land reforms; photo (S)

2000-03-18.title: Ambition Turns to Crisis Control as Clinton Visits the Subcontinent
2000-03-18.abstract: Fall of two Indian governments and nuclear tests of May 1998 have conspired against Pres Clinton's long-held desire to visit India; Clinton will embark on five-day, five-city journey to India, and five-hour stop in Pakistan, at time when tensions on subcontinent are high; confrontation between two countries over kashmir is seen as most likely dispute on earth to spark nuclear clash; Clinton hope his trip can somehow defuse situation in Kashmir, although he and his aides are pessimistic about chances for actual progress; Clinton aides fear that original intention of trip, to display India as economic and strategic power of region, will be undermined by increasingly strong-willed attitude of Indian government toward nuclear weapons, Pakistan and Kashmir (M)

2000-03-18.title: A Political Newcomer Breaks Rules in Germany
2000-03-18.abstract: Angela Merkel, who is about to become leader of Germany's Christian Democrats, will be first woman to head major German party; change she represents is greater than gender: it is religious, generational, moral and geographical; Germany is reaching out for entirely new political blood amid financial scandal that has engulfed Christian Democrats and tarnished reputation of Helmut Kohl, who has admitted accepting more than $1 million in illegal payments; photo (M)

2000-03-18.title: In Clinton, India Sees a Bridegroom
2000-03-18.abstract: India dresses itself up for its first visit from American president in more than two decades; during Pres Clinton's visit, world's largest democracy and its most powerful one will again try to build alliance that has eluded them for half-century; photos (M)

2000-03-19.title: India's Unwired Villages Mired in the Distant Past
2000-03-19.abstract: Illiteracy, sickness and hunger still prevail in India's rural villages like Sheri Ram Reddy Guda, less than 50 miles from software technology park being created in Hyderabad; chasm between India's educated elite and its impoverished multitudes worries economists, politicians and some software entrepreneurs; optimists believe the Internet will eventually help India address problems like illiteracy, but others fear that the boom may be distracting India from its chronic problems and leaving the poor further behind; photos; map (M)

2000-03-19.title: The Holy Land, in an Edgy Mood, Awaits the Pope's Visit
2000-03-19.abstract: Coming visit to Israel by Pope John Paul II is freighted with multiple layers of sensitivities, and each stop on his carefully negotiated itinerary means different things to different people with different agenda; Vatican seeks to accommodate Israeli and Palestinian demands in equal measure; photo (L)

2000-03-19.title: Russian Chief Sees an End To Resistance By Chechens
2000-03-19.abstract: Acting Pres Vladimir V Putin assures Russian voters that war in Chechnya is drawing to a close and that some Russian troops will soon be on their way home; Press Ministry demands that rebels leaders in separatist southern region of Chechnya be denied air time on Russian television and radio stations; those commanders' defiant comments in the media would be an unpleasant reminder that a Russian victory is not yet at hand (M)

2000-03-19.title: Party Undone by Liberty It Nurtured
2000-03-19.abstract: Taiwanese election is a huge defeat for Nationalist Party, which in 20th century controlled first China and then Taiwan and which presided over the emergency of the very democratic forces that ultimately upended it (M)

2000-03-19.title: Nicolas H. Walter Dies at 65; Feisty Atheist and Anarchist
2000-03-19.abstract: Nicolas Hardy Walter, influential British anarchist, atheist and humanist, dies at age 65 (M)

2000-03-20.title: Deep Feeling That Taiwan Is China's Is Put to Test
2000-03-20.abstract: Victory by pro-independence party in Taiwan's presidential elections forces many mainland Chinese to reflect for first time on depth of their convictions, pitting abstract belief that return of Taiwan is manifest destiny, against their personal self-interest, since war would mean renewed hardship in country that has only recently raised living standards (M)

2000-03-20.title: Powerful Israeli Rabbi Steps Up Attacks Over Parochial Schools
2000-03-20.abstract: Rabbi Ovadia Yosef, spiritual leader of Israel's powerful Shas political party, issues blistering and vehement attack in televised sermon against leftist education minister, Yossi Sarid; is incensed over Sarid's perceived attacks against parochial school system, which forms party's political base (S)

2000-03-20.title: Shards of Misery, Not Far From Clinton Stop in Bangladesh
2000-03-20.abstract: Pres Clinton, who will be first American president to visit Bangdladesh, will find country that is still one of world's poorest with per capita income of $289, but one in decidedly in less of mess with poverty rate drifting down and economic growth rates inching up; many Bangladeshi officials express wariness over visit; fear Clinton may be more interested in making deals to exploit country's significant reserves of natural gas than in fostering friendship; map; photos (M)

2000-03-20.title: Ford Plans Job Cuts At Plant in England
2000-03-20.abstract: Ford Motor Co plans layoffs of more than 1,000 workers at giant assembly plant in Dagenham, England, and may close entire factory; says pound's strength relative to euro has made it expensive to manufacture in Britain for sale in Europe (S)

2000-03-20.title: Holy Land Jitters Heighten Security for Papal Visit
2000-03-20.abstract: Unprecedented security preparations by Israel and Palestinian Authority for Pope John Paul II's upcoming six-day visit to the Holy Land discussed; visit has required unparalleled cooperation between two governments, which despite minor sniping of Jerusalem officials on both sides, has gone smoothly; photo (M)

2000-03-21.title: Slipping in Polls, Fujimori Courts Peru's Indians
2000-03-21.abstract: Peru's Pres Alberto K Fujimori, slipping in polls and besieged by international criticism over presidential campaign irregularities, is courting indigenous Indians in flurry of pork-barrel politics; growing number of political experts say for all his efforts he may not win outright majority needed to avert runoff against Alejandro Toledo, probably strongest candidate he has ever faced among poor Indian voters; his image of invincibility has been damaged in recent weeks by widespread perception that he has lost control of election campaign and that election process is badly flawed; photo (M)

2000-03-21.title: 34 Massacred In Sikh Town In Kashmir
2000-03-21.abstract: Band of suspected militants massacre 34 Sikh men in village in Indian state of Kashmir on eve of Pres Clinton's state visit to India, which Clinton had hoped would help bring peace in violence-torn Kashmir; police blame Urdu-speaking foreign mercenaries participating in Pakistan-sponsored efforts to undermine and end rule by India (S)

2000-03-21.title: The Murky Tale of Taiwan: Island With an Unruly Past
2000-03-21.abstract: While China, US and most countries worldwide hold that Taiwan is part of China, its connections with mainland historically have been tenuous; island's relationship with China, dating back to 1683, discussed (S)

2000-03-21.title: U.N. to Review Human Rights Of 2 Big Powers
2000-03-21.abstract: UN Human Rights Commission, which opens annual meeting on March 21, faces crowded docket of supposed violations by number of countries, including China and Russia; US will introduce resolution criticizing China for deteriorating human rights, including crackdown on Falun Gong spiritual movement; commission may be forced to tackle Russia's actions in Chechnya (S)

2000-03-21.title: Colombian Military, in Report, Says Its Rights Abuses Are Down
2000-03-21.abstract: Colombia's minister of defense, Luis Fernando Ramirez Acuna, reports significant decline in human rights violations by country's armed forces, despite reports by State Department and human rights groups to contrary (S)

2000-03-22.title: British Boom Lets Tax Cuts Be the Topic Of Contention
2000-03-22.abstract: LONDON—  The history of Labour governments in Britain has largely been one of economic crises: budget and balance of payments deficits, devaluations, even a bailout by the International Monetary Fund.

2000-03-22.title: NATO Secretary General's Report Claims Successes in Kosovo
2000-03-22.abstract: NATO Sec Gen Lord Robertson, one year after alliance began its 78-day bombardment of Kosovo, issues report highlighting what he says are unsung accomplishments of campaign; cites resettling of 1.3 million refugees, reopening of hundreds of schools and drastic reduction in crime (M)

2000-03-22.title: Ramon Mitra, 72, Foe of Marcos Regime During Martial Law
2000-03-22.abstract: Ramon Mitra, who rose from rural poverty to become leading politician and democratic foe of Marcos dictatorship in Philippines, dies at age 72; photo (M)

2000-03-22.title: Senate Fight Snags Aid Bill For Kosovo And Colombia
2000-03-22.abstract: $9 billion spending bill to help Colombia combat drug traffickers and to pay for US military operations in Kosovo is imperiled because of opposition from Sen Trent Lott and fiscal conservatives in House and Senate (M)

2000-03-22.title: Taiwan Market Shakes Off Vote Results
2000-03-22.abstract: Taiwan stock market jumps 5.5 percent on March 21, more than making up for its 2.6 percent loss on March 20 following victory of opposition leader Chen Shui-bian in presidential election (M)

2000-03-23.title: Assad Meeting With Clinton Could Renew Peace Talks
2000-03-23.abstract: Diplomats say Pres Clinton's scheduled meeting with Pres Hafez al-Assad of Syria could lay groundwork for renewed talks between Syria and Israel, but that peace deal is by no means imminent; Government-controlled Syrian newspapers are calling Assad's meeting with Clinton, in Geneva, a 'last chance' overture to Israel to pledge full Israeli withdrawal from Golan Heights; Israel, under Prime Min Ehud Barak, insists on knowing what kind of peace it will obtain before it agrees unilaterally to withdrawal (M)

2000-03-23.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-03-23.abstract: THE AMERICAS    CANADA: SMOKING BAN VOIDED  British Columbia's Supreme Court struck down one of the toughest anti-smoking rules in the country, a total ban on smoking in bars and restaurants. The court ruled that the provincial Workers' Compensation Board had not adequately consulted with the public before imposing the rule on Jan. 1. The ban resulted in hundreds of layoffs from bars and restaurants, and prompted one rural city to try to join neighboring Alberta.   James Brooke (NYT)    MEXICO: U.S. MILITARY WELCOMED  The chairman of the joint chiefs of staff, Gen. Henry H. Shelton, received an unusually warm welcome from the armed forces during a two-day visit. The defense minister, Gen. Enrique Cervantes Aguirre, decorated him with one of the country's highest military medals, saying he had ''distinguished himself for his respect for our country and its men in arms.'' Mexico's military long regarded the United States as a potential invader, but in recent years the two countries have cooperated in the drug war.   Julia Preston (NYT)

2000-03-23.title: Clinton Fever: A Delighted India Has All the Symptoms
2000-03-23.abstract: Analysis of Pres Clinton's skillful address to Indian Parliament, during which he managed to entertain, flatter and inspire much of country's political leadership, even as he tactfully told them things they would rather not have heard--including United States's disapproval of India's decision to test nuclear weapons in 1998; Clinton seems to be making headway in convincing Indians that US genuinely wants to befriend them, and Clinton mania has seized press as well as ordinary Indians (M)

2000-03-23.title: Taiwan's President Is Expected to Quit Party Post Soon
2000-03-23.abstract: Pres Lee Teng-hui of Taiwan, whose governing Nationalist Party was dealt humiliating electoral defeat, has reportedly agreed to step down as chairman of party; Lee is expected to name Vice Pres Lien Chan, losing Nationalist candidate, as chairman of new reform committee, which will run party as Nationalists cast about for new leaders to revive their discredited party; Lee will remain president until May 20, when he is succeeded by Chen Shui-bian, but losing party chairmanship will deprive him of much of his power (M)

2000-03-23.title: Visit Casts Pope as Ally To 2 Sides In Mideast
2000-03-23.abstract: Pope John Paul II's first visit to Israel and Palestinian territories is officially a personal pilgrimage, but he also comes as head of state who has sought to persuade leaders on both sides that he is crucial temporal ally; to Israeli admirers, he is first pope to extend full recognition to their country; for Palestinian leaders, he is pope who began his papacy by telling United Nations in 1979 of need for settlement of Palestinian question (M)

2000-03-24.title: Smuggling of Iraqi Oil Is Rising, U.N. Is Told
2000-03-24.abstract: Vice Admiral Charles Moore, American coordinator of international fleet that patrols waters off Iraq, tells Security Council committee that steeply rising oil prices have brought huge increase in smuggling of Iraqi oil; diplomats say, after briefing, that Moore tells committee on sanctions against Iraq that much of oil passes through Iranian water; Moore reportedly says that at current rates of oil shipments, Iraq could eaarn up to billion dollars this year in illegal sales; diplomats say profits go directly to Pres Saddam Hussein or those he chooses to reward (S)

2000-03-24.title: Iraq Builds Base for Rebels Fighting Iran, U.S. Contends
2000-03-24.abstract: Senior Clinton administration officials say that Iraq has spent tens of millions of dollars in recent months to build military headquarters for an Iranian opposition group that administration considers a terrorist orgn; officials say Iraqis have used profits from illegal sale of oil to build complex for exclusive use of People's Mujahedeen, Iranian military and political movement whose goal is to overthrow Islamic Republic of Iran (M)

2000-03-24.title: World Briefing
2000-03-24.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: TRIMBLE CHALLENGED -- David Trimble will face a bid to oust him as leader of the Ulster Unionists at a meeting of the party council in Belfast tomorrow from a fierce opponent of the peace accord. Mr. Trimble will be challenged by the Rev. Martin Smyth, above, a former grand master of the militantly Protestant Orange Order. The move threatens further damage to the pact, now stalemated over paramilitary arms.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-03-24.title: Tears of Gratitude, Remembering Poland
2000-03-24.abstract: Mrs Edith Tzirer, Holocaust survivor, is overcome with emotion at Pope John Paul's appearance at Yad Vashem Holocaust memorial, saying she had waited 54 years to thank Pope in person for saving her life in 1945 in small town near Cracow, Poland; photo (M)

2000-03-24.title: Lagging Happily In Russian Race
2000-03-24.abstract: Profile of and interview with Ella Pamfilova, only woman in field of 11 in Russia's presidential election on March 26; photo (M)

2000-03-25.title: Polita Grau, 84; Headed Effort On Behalf of Cuban Children
2000-03-25.abstract: Polita Grau, who helped to spirit thousands of children out of Castro's Cuba, and who then spent 14 years in Cuban jail, accused of being CIA conspirator, dies at age of 84; photos (M)

2000-03-25.title: India Wins Battles in Its War on TB, but It Has a Long Way to Go
2000-03-25.abstract: Pres Clinton visits tuberculosis clinic in Hyderabad, India, where he calls for intensified efforts to control epidemic and to find vaccine that will prevent it; estimated two million people develop tuberculosis each year in India, more than in any other country, and about 450,000 die from it; strategy for coping with disease, which administers special regimen and monitors patients over six- to eight-month period, has been used effectively by government since 1993, but is only available to about 15 percent of population of one billion; has doubled cure rates to 84 percent and cut death rates to 4 percent from 29 percent (M)

2000-03-25.title: Pakistanis Are Uneasy Over Clinton's Visit
2000-03-25.abstract: Pakistanis are uneasy over Pres Clinton's upcoming visit, fearing he will deliver scolding in scheduled television address to nation; many are scornful of Clinton's recent visit to India, where he was warmly greeted, notably in Indian Parliament; Clinton will carry message of 'hard realities;' will tell chief executive Gen Pervez Musharraf country is in trouble and that nuclear-arms race with India would be ruinous folly (M)

2000-03-25.title: Showdown on Irish Pact in Today's Unionist Vote
2000-03-25.abstract: Northern Ireland peace agreement faces crucial vote on March 25 as members of Ulster Unionists decide whether to keep David Trimble as leader or return party to hardline past; 850-member party council will vote on leadership challenge by Rev Martin Smyth, former grand master of militant Protestant Orange Order (S)

2000-03-25.title: State TV Ties a Putin Rival To Jews, Gays and Foreigners
2000-03-25.abstract: Russia's state-owned television broadcasts reports that Grigory A Yavlinsky, leading challenger to Acting Pres Vladimir V Putin, is supported by Jews, homosexuals and foreigners; prime-time reports by ORT television are intended to exploit anti-Semitism, homophobia and suspicion of foreigners that still pervade Russian society, as well as its enthusiasm for conspiracy theories; Yavlisnky, who is running distant third in field of 11 candidates, stands to siphon most votes from Putin; poses greatest danger to Putin's chances of winning mandate in first round of voting; Putin implores Russians to go to polls and to pick leader who can restore country's prestige; photos (M)

2000-03-26.title: Ulster Leader Holds On, but Power Lessens in Unionist Vote
2000-03-26.abstract: David Trimble holds off surprisingly strong challenge to his leadership of Northern Ireland's Ulster Unionist Party, but emerges with his authority and his room to maneuver in conducting peace negotiations diminished; strong vote for Rev Martin Smyth indicates deepening division over going forward with Apr 1998 peace agreement and sharing power with Catholic minority; delegates say they were particularly angered by Trimble's suggestion in Washington on March 17 that he would be willing to take party back into government with Sinn Fein without prior disarmament of Irish Republican Army (M)

2000-03-26.title: Evidence Indicates Uganda Cult Held an Eerie Prelude to Fire
2000-03-26.abstract: Police in Uganda say at least some of 153 people who were killed at secluded compound of Movement for the Restoration of Ten Commandments of God died several weeks before hundreds of followers of doomsday cult died in raging inferno in their church week ago; say fire at compound gutted five small dwellings and scorched much of surrounding earth in eerie prelude to catastrophe that unfolded in Kanungu, where cult made its home; there is growing sense that what happened at compound and in church was carefully orchestrated act by cult's leader, Joseph Kibwetere, and some of his lieutenants, whose whereabouts are unknown (M)

2000-03-26.title: Russians Vote Under the Spell of Putin's Many Promises
2000-03-26.abstract: Pollsters say Russians clearly want Vladimir V Putin to be elected president, although Putin's intentions remain, so far, largely blank slate; Putin's brief campaign has cast him as all things to all people: brutal warrior against Chechens, dog lover who cries at funerals, supporter of basic rights, supporter of crackdown on politically unruly journalists; Putin has managed to remain remarkably popular because he has religiously shunned any one ideology; beyond war in Chechnya, gamble that paid off royally among voters, Putin's actual policies have been few and uncontroversial; photo; map (M)

2000-03-26.title: Rifts Fall Away at Shrine of Annunciation
2000-03-26.abstract: Pope John Paul II is cheered by mixed local crowd of Muslims and Christians in ancient city of Nazareth; he visits Basilica of Annunciation, largest Roman Catholic church in Middle East; Muslims have received approval from Israeli government to build mosque in plaza in front of basilica, decision that has been source of tension in town; photo (M)

2000-03-26.title: OPEC Gives Scant Signs That Oil Flow Will Increase
2000-03-26.abstract: OPEC ministers arriving in Vienna for meeting show few signs of support for big increase in oil production that Clinton administration has asked for to relieve high oil prices; grueling negotations are expected among members, who appear to be divided over issue (M)

2000-03-27.title: Mexico's Ruling Party Accused of Diverting Public Money
2000-03-27.abstract: Mexican prosecutors say they have overwhelming evidence, collected over past two years, that governing Institutional Revolutionary Party leaders engaged in schemes to divert millions of dollars through dummy bank accounts, including three set up under names of two maids and gardener of top party official; Horacio del Norte, party leader in Nuevo Leon, Monterrey State, has admitted using public money for party purposes and state treasurer Xavier Doria Gonzalez, has been jailed for embezzlement and has informed on seven other leaders; map; photo (M)

2000-03-27.title: Washington Nail-Biting
2000-03-27.abstract: News analysis of victory by Vladimir V Putin in Russia's presidential election; likely impact on US-Russian relations assessed; photo (M)

2000-03-27.title: Kuwaitis Are Exasperated By America's Oil Politics
2000-03-27.abstract: Interview with Kuwaiti oil minister Sheik Saud Nasser al-Sabah; he voices displeasure over US pressure to raise production quotas as oil and gasoline prices have soared in recent months (M)

2000-03-27.title: City-Wide Crisis / Overhauling the Pipe Network : Jakarta Lays the Groundwork for Clean Water
2000-03-27.abstract: JAKARTA—  For someone who has lived three decades in a cramped neighborhood of this sprawling capital, Tuminah, a middle-aged woman, remains keenly in touch with nature.

2000-03-27.title: CLINTON'S EFFORT FAILS TO GET SYRIA TO RESUME TALKS
2000-03-27.abstract: Pres Clinton meets with Syrian Pres Hafez al-Assad in Geneva and fails to persuade him to moderate differences with Israel; White House says it is impossible to predict when talks between Israel and Syria will resume; quick end to meeting with Assad dashes Clinton's hopes of presiding over comprehensive peace deal that would incorporate Syria, Palestinians and Israel before he leaves office; failure is met with despair by diplomats on both sides; photo; map (M)

2000-03-28.title: Saudi Arabia Bridles as Rights Group Charges Widespread Abuses
2000-03-28.abstract: Amnesty International issues sharply worded report accusing government of Saudi Arabia of widespread human rights abuses, including arbitrary arrests, torture of detainees and barring of prisoner access to family members or lawyers; Saudi government emphatically rejects findings as biased and inaccurate (M)

2000-03-28.title: Israel Says It Will Restore Seized Land to Arab Village
2000-03-28.abstract: Interior Min Natan Sharansky says he will restore to Israeli Arab village of Kafr Kassem part of its land that was taken decades ago to develop neighboring Jewish town; move and its far-reaching implications discussed; map (M)

2000-03-28.title: Putin Tells Advisers to Prepare 'Weighty' Economic Plan
2000-03-28.abstract: Vladimir V Putin, Russia's president-elect, meets with cabinet day after decisive victory in presidential election; urges it to prepare 'weighty' economic plan; is congratulated by number of prominent world leaders, including Pres Clinton, British Prime Min Tony Blair and Chinese Pres Jiang Zemin; won 53 percent of vote; photo (M)

2000-03-28.title: In Geneva, Clinton Bet That Assad Would Bend, and Lost
2000-03-28.abstract: Senior Clinton administration official says Syrian Pres Hafez al-Assad was immovable during his meeting with Pres Clinton in Geneva, and appears to have arrived with misconception that Mr. Clinton was in position to give him what he wanted from Israel; says administration knew meeting was gamble but believed it had better chance of success than proved to be case; photo (M)

2000-03-28.title: As Pope Exits, Israel Resumes Usual Discord
2000-03-28.abstract: Political discord resumes in Israel following visit by Pope John Paul II; Israelis are pessimistic and anxious as prospects for peace with Syria have diminished, as government opens politically explosive criminal investigation of leading Rabbi Ovadia Yosef and amid five separate Parliament votes of no confidence in government; Yosef, who attacked Education Minister Yossi Sarid in recent sermon, is accused of 'praising or encouraging violence' as well as insulting public employee and slander (M)

2000-03-29.title: Ex-Official Challenges China's Limits on Speech
2000-03-29.abstract: Bao Tong, former senior Chinese official who spent seven years in prison for sympathizing with democracy advocates, challenges authorities to abide by the Constitution and stop restricting his freedom to speak out; photo (M)

2000-03-29.title: WITH IRAN BALKING, OPEC NATIONS PLAN TO SELL MORE OIL
2000-03-29.abstract: OPEC decides to increase oil production without the participation of its second-largest producer, Iran, but the increase, 1.45 million barrels a day, is less than the 2.5 million urged by US; Iran's irritation at US pressures is apparently one reason behind its opposition to any increase; experts estimate that it will take at least six to eight weeks before any additional oil production finds its way to the gasoline pumps; Clinton administration remains optimistic, citing additional production it expects from non-OPEC producers; graph; photos (M)

2000-03-29.title: Israeli Police Urge Prosecution of Netanyahu and Wife
2000-03-29.abstract: Israeli police recommend that former Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu and his wife, Sara, face criminal prosecution on charges of bribery, fraud and the theft of $100,000 worth of gifts to the state; Netanyahu says charges are baseless, and his lawyer says he welcomes the trial; photo (M)

2000-03-29.title: On Southeast Asia Tour,Musharraf Hears StrongWords on Civilian Rule : Mahathir Presses Pakistani on Democracy
2000-03-29.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  The prime minister of Malaysia, Mahathir bin Mohamad, delivered unusually firm words on the importance of democracy Tuesday in a meeting with Pakistan's military leader, General Pervez Musharraf.

2000-03-29.title: Turkey to Say if Leader Can Seek 2nd Term
2000-03-29.abstract: Turkish Parliament is about to begin delicate work of deciding whether to re-elect President Suleyman Demirel, who is maneuvering to arrange a constitutional amendment that would allow him to serve for another five years; re-election is certain if amendment wins; his supporters say Demirel is a guarantee of stability as Turkey enters a decade that is likely to bring wrenching domestic and international changes; critics view him as symbol of country's political immobility (M)

2000-03-30.title: Nationalist Named Taiwan's Prime Minister
2000-03-30.abstract: Pres-elect Chen Shui-bian of Taiwan chooses departing Nationalist defense minister, Tang Fei, as prime minister in his government, which takes office on May 20; Tang's appointment may help persuade Beijing that Chen does not intend to make radical changes in policy toward mainland (S)

2000-03-30.title: Between Migrants and Spain: The Sea That Kills
2000-03-30.abstract: Spain is increasingly a magnet for Africans hoping to find jobs in Europe; Spanish police boats regularly patrol Mediterranean, in hope of intercepting small motorboats crowded with clandestine African migrants, mostly young men from Morocco who want to sneak ashore and hunt for work in Europe; tens of thousands of undocumented migrants are taking this route every year, drawn by booming market in low-paying jobs that Europeans do not want; many of their flimsy boats sink, and Spanish Civil Guard regularly finds bloated bodies in water, or flotsam from boats; European Union has tried to enlist Morocco to stem flow of people coming illegally, but European diplomats in Morocco say government there is ignoring problem; photo; maps (M)

2000-03-30.title: Wahid Seeks Inquiry Into 1960s Killings In Indonesia
2000-03-30.abstract: JAKARTA—  The president of Indonesia, Abdurrahman Wahid, is pressing for an end to a long-standing ban on the Indonesian Communist Party and a judicial inquiry into the killing of hundreds of thousands of alleged Communists in the 1960s when former President Suharto took power, despite warnings that such steps could cause a military backlash.

2000-03-30.title: House Adds $4 Billion to Pentagon Budget
2000-03-30.abstract: House approves $4 billion in additional Pentagon spending for year, and lawmakers edge closer to approving $12.6 billion overall package that would also help Colombia combat drug trafficking and pay for military operations in Kosovo (M)

2000-03-30.title: Russia Arrests Officer in Case Of the Killing Of a Chechen
2000-03-30.abstract: Russian officer is charged with killing civilian in Chechnya, in rare move by Russian military to crack down on human rights abuses; Gen Anatoly Kvashnin, chief of Russian General Staff, says Russian troops apologized to family of victim, a woman, and expressed regrets by attending her funeral; international human rights organizations have reported numerous cases in which Chechens have been robbed, beaten, killed and raped, but there has been little or no action until now by authorities to bring culprits to justice; Rachel Denber of Human Rights Watch calls for thorough investigation of massacres that have been documented; curbing rights abuses is important for Russia's effort to maintain smooth relations with West, and also for its longer-term effort to build credibility with Chechen people and win peace (M)

2000-03-31.title: World Briefing
2000-03-31.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    IRAN: TALIBAN FOE FLEES -- Iran's official news agency said Ismail Khan, the former governor of Herat Province in Afghanistan and a major figure in the opposition to the ruling Taliban, had crossed into Iran and reached the city of Meshed after escaping from a Taliban prison. Mr. Khan, a hero to many Afghans for his fight against Soviet forces in the 1980's, was said to have reached Iran with another anti-Taliban leader who escaped from prison with him, Hafiz Qadir. Iran strongly opposes the Taliban.   John F. Burns (NYT)

2000-03-31.title: Talks With Europeans End
2000-03-31.abstract: Talks in Beijing break off without a deal between China and European Union over terms of China's entry into World Trade Orgn (S)

2000-03-31.title: In Charmed India, Clinton Wooed, and Maybe Won
2000-03-31.abstract: Pres Clinton, without winning or conceding anything of substance on his tour of South Asia, may still have helped to modestly reduce risk of war between nuclear-armed India and Pakistan by talking sweet to India and tough to Pakistan (News Analysis) (M)

2000-03-31.title: Rudolf Kirchschlager, 85, Judge Who Became Austria's President
2000-03-31.abstract: Rudolf Kirchschlager, popular and widely respected president of Austria from 1974 to 1986, dies of heart attack at 85; portrait (S)

2000-03-31.title: 2 Days of U.S.-China Talks Said to Aid Relations
2000-03-31.abstract: Samuel R Berger, White House national security adviser, completes two days of talks in Beijing that an American official describes as 'serious and thoughtful'; senior official says that United States reaffirms to China its support for 'one China,' but also stresses need for peaceful dialogue between Beijing and Taipei (M)
